methodology_sections: 1. Conservation Laws - Solvency, inflation checks, supply integrity
2. Relationship & Ratio Invariants - Collateralization, rate consistency
3. Economic Monotonicity - Yield accumulation, exchange rate direction
4. Incentive & Game Theoretic Integrity - Attack cost vs profit, slashing
5. Fee & Yield Consistency - Fee accounting, yield distribution

invariants.description: The TaikoL1 contract's TKO balance must always be greater than or equal to the sum of all active liveness bonds, validity bonds, and contest bonds for all unverified blocks.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the contract does not hold enough TKO, provers and contesters cannot withdraw their bonds when blocks are verified, leading to a loss of funds and a halt in the proving process.
invariants.invariant_logic: tko.balanceOf(address(TaikoL1)) >= sum(blocks[i].livenessBond) + sum(transitions[i][j].validityBond) + sum(transitions[i][j].contestBond) for all i in (lastVerifiedBlockId, numBlocks]
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_tko_solvency() public view { assert(tko.balanceOf(address(this)) >= totalActiveBonds); }

invariants.description: During ETH deposit processing, the sum of the amounts of the processed deposits plus the total fee extracted must exactly equal the sum of the original deposit amounts.
invariants.attack_scenario: If fees are calculated incorrectly, ETH could be minted out of thin air on L2, or user deposits could be silently burned without being credited to the fee recipient.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_deposit_conservation(uint256[] memory originalAmounts, uint256 totalFee, uint256[] memory processedAmounts) public pure { uint256 sumProcessed; for(uint i=0; i<processedAmounts.length; i++) sumProcessed += processedAmounts[i]; assert(sumProcessed + totalFee == sum(originalAmounts)); }

invariants.description: The tier of a transition for a given block can only increase. A transition's tier cannot be downgraded by a new proof.
invariants.attack_scenario: If an attacker can submit a lower-tier proof to overwrite a higher-tier proof, they could bypass the security guarantees of the higher tier (e.g., SGX or Guardian) and force an invalid state transition.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_tier_monotonicity(uint64 slot, uint32 tid, uint16 newTier) public view { assert(newTier >= state.transitions[slot][tid].tier); }

invariants.description: When a transition is contested and subsequently overwritten by a higher-tier proof, the reward paid to the winner must be strictly less than or equal to the slashed bond of the loser.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the reward exceeds the slashed bond, attackers could profitably self-contest their own proofs to drain the protocol's TKO treasury.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_slashing_reward(uint256 reward, uint256 slashedBond) public pure { assert(reward <= slashedBond); }

invariants.description: The lastVerifiedBlockId must be strictly monotonically increasing and can never decrease.
invariants.attack_scenario: If lastVerifiedBlockId decreases, previously verified blocks could be re-verified, leading to double-spending of bonds, replay of state roots, and catastrophic L2 state desync.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_last_verified_block_monotonicity(uint64 newLastVerifiedBlockId) public view { assert(newLastVerifiedBlockId >= state.slotB.lastVerifiedBlockId); }

invariants.description: The total TKO returned to a prover during block verification must not exceed the sum of the transition's validity bond and the block's liveness bond.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the payout exceeds the deposited bonds, the protocol would leak TKO, eventually becoming insolvent and unable to pay other provers.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_payout_cap(uint256 bondToReturn, uint96 validityBond, uint96 livenessBond) public pure { assert(bondToReturn <= uint256(validityBond) + uint256(livenessBond)); }

invariants.description: The number of proposed but unverified blocks must never exceed blockMaxProposals, ensuring the ring buffer never overwrites an unverified block.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the ring buffer overwrites an unverified block, the liveness bond and state transition data for that block are lost, permanently locking the prover's funds and breaking the L2 chain progression.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_ring_buffer_safety() public view { assert(state.slotB.numBlocks - state.slotB.lastVerifiedBlockId <= getConfig().blockMaxProposals + 1); }
